task_completion.description: Navigate to the subject record for DM-105 within the DM-TRIAL-2024 study.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the clinical trial application interface (e.g., OpenClinica) with the active study set to 'DM-TRIAL-2024' and the subject matrix or record for 'DM-105' open.

task_completion.description: Restore the previously removed 'Month 3 Follow-up' event for subject DM-105.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Month 3 Follow-up' event for DM-105 should no longer appear as 'removed', 'deleted', or 'invalid'. It should have an active status icon (e.g., scheduled, data entry started, or available) in the subject's event grid.

task_completion.description: Schedule the 'Month 6 Follow-up' event for subject DM-105.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Month 6 Follow-up' event should appear in the subject's event matrix or schedule list with a status indicating it has been scheduled.

task_completion.description: Set the Start Date of the 'Month 6 Follow-up' event to 2024-06-01.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A scheduling form, event summary, or subject matrix should explicitly display the date '2024-06-01' associated with the 'Month 6 Follow-up' event for DM-105.

task_completion.description: Add a Discrepancy Note to DM-105's subject record.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A discrepancy note icon, thread, or popup window should be visible and attached to the subject record or study event for DM-105.

task_completion.description: Ensure the Discrepancy Note is of type 'Annotation' and contains the correct description.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The discrepancy note details must show the Type as 'Annotation' and the description or detailed note text must read 'Restored Month 3 event per sponsor'.

integrity.description: The agent must use the web application's graphical user interface to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser interacting with the clinical trial software. There should be no evidence of terminal windows executing SQL scripts, curl commands, or direct database modifications.

integrity.description: The results must be displayed within the actual clinical trial software interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The scheduled dates, restored events, and discrepancy notes must be rendered as HTML elements within the application's tables or forms, not just typed into a text editor or notepad.

integrity.description: The agent should not use browser developer tools to fake the visual output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's developer console or 'Inspect Element' panel should not be open and actively modifying the DOM to fake the presence of the restored event, scheduled date, or discrepancy note.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target study identifier is exactly 'DM-TRIAL-2024'. Target subject ID is exactly 'DM-105'. The Month 6 Follow-up start date must be exactly '2024-06-01'. The discrepancy note must contain the text 'Restored Month 3' (case-insensitive check is acceptable for the VLM, though the exact requested string is 'Restored Month 3 event per sponsor').
